title: spergula rubra subsp. tenuifolia
intro: Spergula rubra subsp. tenuifolia, also known as sand spurry or creeping sand spurry, is a flowering plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving kidney function, as well as being used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Spergula rubra subsp. tenuifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
